title: Lesson 6 – Actions and Invitations
description: Aktionen mit を und で: Objektpartikel を, Ortspartikel で, Einladungen mit ませんか und Vorschläge mit ましょう.

grammar.title: N を V ます (Object Particle を)
grammar.explanation: The particle を indicates the object of a transitive verb. It marks the noun that the action is performed on. The pronunciation of を is 'o', the same as お, but it is written as を when used as a particle.
grammar.example_sentences:
① ジュースを 飲みます。
  (Juusu o nomimasu.)
  — I drink juice.
② 新聞を 読みます。
  (Shinbun o yomimasu.)
  — I read a newspaper.

grammar.title: Place で V ます (Location Particle で)
grammar.explanation: The particle で indicates the place where an action takes place. It tells us where the activity described by the verb is performed. Note: で is used for the location of actions, not for the location of existence (which uses に).
grammar.example_sentences:
③ レストランで 昼ご飯を 食べます。
  (Resutoran de hirugohan o tabemasu.)
  — I eat lunch at a restaurant.
④ うちで 映画を 見ます。
  (Uchi de eiga o mimasu.)
  — I watch a movie at home.

grammar.title: V ませんか (Invitation)
grammar.explanation: V ませんか is used to invite someone to do something. It literally means 'Won't you...?' but functions as a polite invitation. It is softer and more polite than the direct imperative form.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑤ 一緒に 昼ご飯を 食べませんか。
  (Issho ni hirugohan o tabemasen ka.)
  — Won't you eat lunch together (with me)?
⑥ 一緒に コーヒーを 飲みませんか。
  (Issho ni koohii o nomimasen ka.)
  — Would you like to have coffee together?

grammar.title: V ましょう (Let's ~)
grammar.explanation: V ましょう is used to suggest doing something together. It is used when the speaker wants to positively invite the listener to do something. It can also be used as a response to an invitation with V ませんか, meaning 'Yes, let's do that.'
grammar.example_sentences:
⑦ ちょっと 休みましょう。
  (Chotto yasumimashou.)
  — Let's take a short break.
⑧ 一緒に 映画を 見ましょう。
  (Issho ni eiga o mimashou.)
  — Let's watch a movie together.

conversation.title: Shall we eat together?
conversation.title_ja: 一緒に 食べませんか

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: 田中さんは 毎日 どこで 昼ご飯を 食べますか。
lines.romaji: Tanaka-san wa mainichi doko de hirugohan o tabemasu ka.
lines.english: Tanaka, where do you eat lunch every day?

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: いつも 会社の 食堂で 食べます。山本さんは？
lines.romaji: Itsumo kaisha no shokudou de tabemasu. Yamamoto-san wa?
lines.english: I always eat at the company cafeteria. How about you, Yamamoto?

lines.speaker: Yamamoto
lines.japanese: わたしは ときどき あの レストランで 食べます。
lines.romaji: Watashi wa tokidoki ano resutoran de tabemasu.
lines.english: I sometimes eat at that restaurant.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: そうですか。何を 食べますか。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ka. Nani o tabemasu ka.
lines.english: Is that so? What do you eat?

lines.speaker: Yamamoto
lines.japanese: 魚と 野菜を 食べます。おいしいですよ。
lines.romaji: Sakana to yasai o tabemasu. Oishii desu yo.
lines.english: I eat fish and vegetables. It's delicious.

lines.speaker: Yamamoto
lines.japanese: 今日 一緒に 食べませんか。
lines.english: Would you like to eat together today?
